skill.name: phenotype-introduction
skill.description: Expert system for generating comprehensive biomedical phenotype introductions with structured academic content. Use when users request detailed explanations of cellular phenotypes including concept, mechanism, regulation, and detection methods in Chinese academic writing.
skill.summary: Generates structured four-section biomedical phenotype introductions with word count validation for Chinese academic research writing.
skill.author: AIPOCH

security_audit.summary: All 48 static analysis findings were evaluated and determined to be false positives. The skill is a legitimate academic writing tool for generating structured biomedical phenotype introductions in Chinese. Detected patterns (markdown backticks flagged as shell execution, placeholder text flagged as cryptographic, source URL flagged as network reconnaissance) are safe usage patterns within an academic content generation context. No prompt injection, data exfiltration, or malicious intent detected.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm Pattern - False Positive
low_findings.description: Static scanner flagged 24 instances of 'weak cryptographic algorithm' across multiple files. All instances are false positives: placeholder file text (example_asset.txt), JSON audit report field values (phenotype-introduction_audit_result_v1.json), markdown documentation templates (api_reference.md, academic_writing_guide.md), sample Chinese text strings in validation script (validate_word_count.py), and YAML frontmatter/instructional content (SKILL.md). No actual cryptographic operations exist in the skill.

low_findings.title: Shell Backtick Execution Pattern - False Positive
low_findings.description: Static scanner flagged 20 instances of 'Ruby/shell backtick execution' in SKILL.md and academic_writing_guide.md. All instances are markdown inline code formatting (single backticks around file paths and variable names) and markdown code blocks (triple backticks showing example commands and output format). No actual shell execution occurs. These are documentation conventions for a content-generation skill.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded URL Pattern - False Positive
low_findings.description: Static scanner flagged a GitHub repository URL in SKILL.md line 7. This is a source attribution link to the skill's repository (https://github.com/aipoch/medical-research-skills). It is a legitimate reference, not a data exfiltration endpoint or malicious network call.

low_findings.title: Unicode Escape Sequence Pattern - False Positive
low_findings.description: Static scanner flagged Unicode escape sequences in validate_word_count.py line 12. The pattern r'[\u4e00-\u9fff]' is a legitimate Python regex for matching Chinese character ranges in a word count validation script. No obfuscation or encoding evasion intended.

low_findings.title: System Reconnaissance Pattern - False Positive
low_findings.description: Static scanner flagged 5 instances of 'system reconnaissance' in audit result JSON, academic_writing_guide.md, and SKILL.md. All instances are legitimate content: quality standards descriptions for academic writing, structured JSON audit data, and parameter clarification guidance for phenotype content generation. No actual system enumeration or reconnaissance activity.

content.user_title: Generate Structured Biomedical Phenotype Introductions for Research
content.value_statement: Researchers need detailed, well-organized phenotype introductions for Chinese academic writing but struggle with consistent structure and word count compliance. This skill generates comprehensive biomedical phenotype introductions with four mandatory sections covering concept, mechanism, regulation, and marker detection methods.
actual_capabilities: Generates detailed Concept section with definition, biological characteristics, and historical background for any cellular phenotype
Creates Mechanism section describing occurrence processes, cellular impacts, and key molecular components
Produces Regulation section covering signaling pathways, regulatory principles, and phenotype crosstalk
Generates Marker Detection section with minimum 5 key markers, detection principles, and methods
Validates output against word count requirements using a dedicated Python validation script
Formats content in strict academic structure with correct Chinese biomedical terminology
limitations: Content is generated as academic text and is not experimentally validated research data
Word counts are approximate for Chinese text using character-based counting methods
Only covers biomedical cellular phenotypes, not other scientific or non-biomedical domains
Output quality depends on the specificity and accuracy of the phenotype name provided by the user

use_cases.title: Academic Research Paper Writing
use_cases.description: A biomedical researcher writing a Chinese academic paper needs a comprehensive introduction section for a cellular phenotype. The skill generates structured content covering all required sections with proper terminology.

use_cases.title: Literature Review Preparation
use_cases.description: A graduate student preparing a literature review on cell death mechanisms needs detailed phenotype explanations. The skill produces organized content describing phenotype mechanisms, regulation, and markers.

use_cases.title: Teaching Material Creation
use_cases.description: An instructor creating course materials for cell biology needs clear, structured explanations of cellular phenotypes. The skill generates educational content with consistent academic formatting.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Phenotype Introduction Request
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a phenotype introduction for [phenotype name].
prompt_templates.scenario: Simple request for a complete four-section phenotype introduction

prompt_templates.title: Mechanism-Focused Query
prompt_templates.prompt: Explain the mechanism of [phenotype name] including key molecular components and signaling pathways involved in its occurrence process.
prompt_templates.scenario: User needs detailed mechanism information for a specific phenotype

prompt_templates.title: Full Academic Introduction
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a comprehensive introduction for [phenotype name] with all four sections: concept, mechanism, regulation, and marker detection methods. Output in Chinese with all section word count requirements satisfied.
prompt_templates.scenario: Full academic writing task with complete structure requirements

prompt_templates.title: Advanced Phenotype Comparison Request
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a detailed phenotype introduction for [phenotype name] with emphasis on its crosstalk with [related phenotype]. Include at least 5 marker molecules with detection principles and methods. Validate that each section meets minimum word count requirements.
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced request requiring phenotype interaction analysis and validation

output_examples.input: Generate a phenotype introduction for Pyroptosis.
output_examples.output: A four-section academic text in Chinese covering pyroptosis concept (definition as caspase-1 mediated inflammatory cell death, biological characteristics, historical discovery), mechanism (occurrence process, molecular components including GSDMD and inflammasomes, cellular impacts), regulation (signaling pathways, inflammatory response control, crosstalk with apoptosis), and marker detection (GSDMD, Caspase-1, IL-1beta, IL-18, PI staining with detection methods).

output_examples.input: Explain Ferroptosis regulation including detection markers with minimum 5 markers.
output_examples.output: A regulation-focused academic section in Chinese describing iron-dependent cell death regulatory mechanisms, GPX4 and glutathione pathway, lipid peroxidation process, feedback loops, crosstalk with apoptosis and necroptosis, and five key markers (GPX4, ACSL4, 4-HNE, MDA, Fe2+) with detection principles and methods such as Western blot and immunofluorescence.

best_practices: Specify the exact phenotype name clearly to ensure accurate content generation with correct terminology
Review all generated content for scientific accuracy and verify terminology before academic use
Use the validate_word_count.py script to verify each section meets minimum word count requirements
anti_patterns: Requesting a phenotype introduction without specifying the exact phenotype name, leading to generic or incorrect content
Using generated content as primary experimental research data instead of reference academic text
Requesting content on multiple unrelated phenotypes in a single request, producing insufficient depth for each

faq.question: What is a phenotype introduction?
faq.answer: A structured academic document that explains a biomedical cellular phenotype including its concept, mechanism, regulation, and detection methods.

faq.question: What phenotypes can this skill generate content for?
faq.answer: This skill supports biomedical cellular phenotypes including cell death types, cellular stress responses, and metabolic processes.

faq.question: What language is the output written in?
faq.answer: The output is in Simplified Chinese with precise biomedical terminology and English terms provided in parentheses.

faq.question: How does the word count validation work?
faq.answer: The validate_word_count.py script counts Chinese characters as an approximation of word count for each of the four sections.

faq.question: Can I customize the output structure?
faq.answer: The output follows a fixed four-section structure. You can request emphasis on specific sections for your needs.

faq.question: Is the generated content scientifically validated?
faq.answer: No. The content is AI-generated academic text and should be reviewed for scientific accuracy before use.
